name: factorial
description: Access Factorial HR employees, teams, and time-off data, and create leave requests

requiredApis.name: Factorial API key (created by an administrator in the Factorial platform)

envVars.name: FACTORIAL_API_KEY
envVars.description: Factorial API key, sent without any prefix in the x-api-key header. API keys grant total access and never expire, so store securely.

tools.name: List Employees
tools.description: List employees in the Factorial account, with filters for name, activity status, and team

only_managers.description: Return only employees who are managers

tools.name: Get Employee
tools.description: Get a single Factorial employee record by ID

tools.name: List Leaves
tools.description: List time-off leaves (absences) with filters for employee, date range, and approval status

include_deleted_leaves.description: Whether to include deleted leave records

from.description: Only leaves starting on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

to.description: Only leaves up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

include_leave_type.description: Include leave type information in the response

include_duration.description: Include computed leave duration in the response

tools.name: Create Leave
tools.description: Create a time-off leave (absence) for an employee, such as vacation or sick leave

employee_id.description: Factorial employee ID the leave belongs to

leave_type_id.description: Leave type ID (for example vacation or sick leave) configured in Factorial

description.description: Free-text reason or note for the leave

half_day.description: Mark the leave as a half day: beginning_of_day or end_of_day

tools.name: List Teams
tools.description: List all teams in the Factorial account, useful before filtering employees by team

tools.name: List Leave Types
tools.description: List the leave types configured in the account (vacation, sick leave, ...) with their IDs — required to pick a valid leave_type_id for Create Leave

active.description: Only active (true) or inactive (false) leave types

employee_id.description: Only leave types available to this employee

tools.name: Delete Leave
tools.description: Delete (cancel) a leave request by its ID

prompts.title: Who is out this week
prompts.prompt: List Factorial leaves overlapping the current week and summarize who is away, for how long, and whether the leave is approved.

prompts.title: Team roster
prompts.prompt: List Factorial teams, then list the active employees of a team I pick and summarize the roster.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Factorial setup

steps.title: Get a Factorial account with admin access
steps.description: Sign up at factorialhr.com (a free trial is available) or use an existing company account. Only administrators with full permissions can create and manage API keys.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: As an administrator, generate an API key from the Factorial platform user interface (see https://help.factorialhr.com/how-to-create-api-keys-in-factorial). Copy the key value.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Set the key as FACTORIAL_API_KEY. It is sent as-is in the x-api-key header, without any prefix.

notes: Factorial API keys grant TOTAL access to the account and never expire — rotate them carefully and keep them secret.
The API is date-versioned per quarter (this connector uses the 2026-04-01 version); each version is supported for one year after release.
Regular (non-admin) users see a restricted version of employee data; admin-generated keys see everything.
